title: Scaffold Hono backend and Docker environment
body:
- Create a `src/server` directory for the Hono backend.
- Add a `docker-compose.yml` in the root with PostgreSQL 16 using the `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` image.
- **Add a volume mount so the backend can access the user's notes directory:**
```yaml
volumes:
  - ${HOME}/NoteShark:/notes:ro
```
- Set the notes path via environment variable: `NOTES_DIR=/notes` (used by the indexing pipeline in the chokidar watcher).
- Configure a `Dockerfile` for the Hono server using `node:20-alpine`.
- Set up Prisma with the `postgresql` provider and enable `previewFeatures = ["postgresqlExtensions"]` to support `pgvector`.
- Define a `DocumentChunk` model in `schema.prisma` with: `id`, `noteTitle`, `content`, `embedding` (Unsupported("vector(768)")), and `metadata` (JSONB).
- Create a basic Hono app in `src/server/index.ts` with a `/health` endpoint and CORS enabled for `localhost:5173`.

title: Implement Sidecar process management in Electron
body:
In `src/main/lib/server.ts`:
- Implement `startBackendServer()`: uses `child_process.spawn` to run `docker-compose up` or the compiled Hono binary.
- Map logs from the sidecar to the Electron main process console with a `[BACKEND]` prefix.
- **Do NOT block the renderer while the backend boots.** The editor must be fully interactive immediately on launch. Backend boot is async and non-blocking.
- Run the heartbeat check (`/health` polling) silently in the background after the renderer loads. When the backend is ready, resolve `backendReady` and enable AI features.
- Register an `app.on('before-quit')` handler to ensure `docker-compose down` or `child_process.kill()` is called, preventing orphaned background processes.
- Expose `getBackendStatus` via IPC to show connection state as a non-blocking status indicator in the UI (e.g., a small dot in the toolbar — not a loading screen).

title: Set up AI integration and configuration UI
body:
- **Do NOT install `@google/genai`.** OpenRouter uses an OpenAI-compatible API. Install the OpenAI SDK pointed at OpenRouter's base URL instead:
```bash
pnpm add openai
```
  Configure the client as:
```ts
import OpenAI from 'openai';
const client = new OpenAI({
  baseURL: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1',
  apiKey: process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY,
});
```
  This handles both chat completions and embeddings via OpenRouter's unified endpoint. Ensure `openai` is listed in `package.json` and installed inside the Docker image.
- **Add** new AI model constants in `src/shared/constants.ts` (note: the existing file only has `appDirectoryName`, `fileEncoding`, and `autoSavingTime`; do not replace anything, just add):
```ts
export const AI_MODELS = {
  // Verify all IDs at https://openrouter.ai/models before use
  EMBED:  'google/gemma-3-12b-it',      // Smallest Gemma — used for embeddings endpoint
  FAST:   'google/gemma-3-12b-it',      // Mood analysis, intent detection
  HEAVY:  'google/gemma-3-27b-it',      // RAG augmentation, summaries
  PRO:    'google/gemma-4-27b-it',      // Council of Thought: Visionary (corrected — Gemma 4 tops at 27B, not 31B)
  FLASH:  'google/gemma-4-27b-it:free', // Council of Thought: Pragmatist — swap for a verified smaller Gemma 4 variant if available
} as const;
```
  **Important:** Cross-check every model ID against the live OpenRouter catalog before merging. The Gemma 4 family in particular changes frequently. If a dedicated Gemma embedding model becomes available on OpenRouter, prefer it for `EMBED`.
- Build a `SettingsModal` component in the renderer with:
  - API Key input (OpenRouter key)
  - Model selector dropdown using the constants above
- Persist the API key securely using `electron-store` with encryption enabled.
- **Secret pipeline**: Electron main process reads the key from `electron-store` and passes it to the backend:
  - If Docker: inject via `environment:` block in `docker-compose` or a temporary `.env` file written at startup
  - If binary: set `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in the child process environment when spawning

title: Build the indexing pipeline (Total Recall)
body:
- In the Hono server, implement a `POST /index` endpoint.
- Use `chokidar` in the backend to watch the path at `process.env.NOTES_DIR` (mapped to `~/NoteShark/` on the host via the Docker volume mount defined in the scaffold issue — **do not hardcode `~/NoteShark/`; the container cannot resolve host home paths without the volume**).
- Implementation: When a file is saved, read the content, strip Markdown frontmatter, and use a recursive character splitter (target size 1000 chars, 200 overlap).
- Call OpenRouter's embeddings endpoint using the `openai` SDK client: `client.embeddings.create({ model: AI_MODELS.EMBED, input: chunk })` for each chunk.
- Upsert the chunks into PostgreSQL, using the filename as a unique identifier to clear old chunks before re-indexing.

title: Implement Semantic Search (RAG) endpoint
body:
- Create a `GET /search` endpoint in Hono that accepts a `q` query parameter.
- Implementation: Embed the user query string using `AI_MODELS.EMBED`, then perform a vector similarity search using Prisma's `$queryRaw` with the **`<=>` (cosine distance) operator** — not `<->` (L2). Cosine similarity is correct for text embeddings because semantic meaning is encoded in vector direction, not magnitude.
```sql
SELECT id, "noteTitle", content
FROM "DocumentChunk"
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
LIMIT 5;
```
- Return the top 5 most relevant chunks with their parent note title and a small context snippet.
- In the renderer, create a `useRecall` hook that triggers this search as the user types (debounced) and exposes the results.

title: Implement unified Mood + Intent analysis engine
body:
- In the Hono server, create a single `POST /analyze` endpoint that returns **both mood and intent in one LLM call** — do not make two separate API calls on the same trigger.
- Prompt Strategy: Send the last 500 characters of the current note to `google/gemma-3-12b-it` with a system prompt to return a single JSON object:
```json
{
  "mood": "venting" | "focused" | "creative",
  "confidence": number,
  "reasoning": string,
  "toolTrigger": null | {
    "type": "planning" | "summarize" | "checklist",
    "hint": string
  }
}
```
- In `useMarkdownEditor.tsx`: Add a debounce (5000ms) that sends the editor content to `/analyze`.
- Update `currentMoodAtom` in Jotai with the `mood` field.
- If `toolTrigger` is non-null, dispatch to the `ToolOverlay` component (see Intent Tooling issue).
- **Rationale:** Collapsing mood and intent into one prompt halves API calls and latency. The model context is identical for both tasks — there is no benefit to splitting them.

title: UI: Mood-Aware Interface transitions
body:
- In `index.css`: Define CSS variables for mood themes (e.g., `--mood-accent: #f87171` for venting/stress, `--mood-accent: #34d399` for focus).
- Create a `MoodStatusBar` component that displays a small emoji and the current mental state at the bottom of the editor.
- Implement a vanilla CSS transition that shifts the border-glow of the `MarkdownEditor` and the background of the `DraggableTopBar` based on the `currentMoodAtom`.

title: Build Dynamic Tooling: Intent Overlay
body:
- The `toolTrigger` field is now provided by the unified `/analyze` endpoint (see Mood + Intent analysis issue). This issue covers the renderer-side only.
- Create a `ToolOverlay` component that appears near the cursor or in a side-panel when `toolTrigger` is non-null in the Jotai atom.
- The overlay displays a contextual action based on `toolTrigger.type`:
  - `planning`: Offer to generate a task list or simple timeline from the current paragraph.
  - `summarize`: Offer a one-paragraph summary of the current note.
  - `checklist`: Extract action items and offer to write them back as a Markdown checklist.
- Each action includes a 'Write to Note' button that uses `window.context.writeNote` to append the generated content.
- The overlay auto-dismisses after 8 seconds if not interacted with.

title: Implement Council of Thought: Orchestrator
body:
- Create a `POST /debate` endpoint in Hono.
- Use the model constants defined in `src/shared/constants.ts`. The three personas map as follows:
  - **The Visionary**: `AI_MODELS.PRO` (`google/gemma-4-27b-it`) — expansive, generative thinking
  - **The Skeptic**: `AI_MODELS.HEAVY` (`google/gemma-3-27b-it`) — critical, finds holes
  - **The Pragmatist**: `AI_MODELS.FLASH` (`google/gemma-4-27b-it:free` or verified smaller Gemma 4 variant) — concrete, actionable
- Implementation: Launch three parallel `fetch` requests to OpenRouter using the `openai` SDK client (same client instance configured in phase-6).
- Use Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream the three responses back to the client simultaneously.
- **Error handling**: If any one of the three model calls fails mid-stream, emit an `error` SSE event for that persona's stream and continue streaming the other two. The client must handle partial results gracefully — a failed persona shows a fallback message, not a broken panel.

title: UI: Council of Thought Debate Panel
body:
- Build a `DebatePanel` component that slides in from the right when text is highlighted and a 'Debate' action button is clicked.
- Render three message bubbles that update in real-time as the stream progresses.
- Handle the per-persona error state from the SSE stream: if a persona's stream emits an error event, show a muted 'Unavailable' state for that bubble without breaking the other two.
- Each bubble should have a 'Summarize to Note' button that takes the best points from that persona and appends them to the current Markdown file as a blockquote.

title: Final Integration and Performance Tuning
body:
- Add a non-blocking backend status indicator to the Electron startup sequence (e.g., a small animated dot in the toolbar showing 'Thinking environment loading...'). Do not use a blocking progress bar — the editor must be usable immediately per the sidecar issue.
- **'Local First, AI Second'** is the invariant: the editor, file system, and all non-AI features must be fully functional even if the Docker backend never starts.
- Optimize the `pgvector` index by adding an HNSW index once the note count exceeds 1000 chunks:
```sql
CREATE INDEX ON "DocumentChunk" USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops);
```
  Note: `vector_cosine_ops` must match the `<=>` operator used in queries.
- **Production binary bundling**: This needs a dedicated investigation spike. Packaging a Hono server binary inside an Electron app is non-trivial. Recommended tooling: `pkg` or `caxa` for compiling the Node server to a standalone binary. Add an explicit issue for this spike before phase-11 begins — 'Verify build scripts' is not enough specification to ship against.
